commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f4637989362dc8fdd87c930d7270fb8383428d6d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes shared message action normalization so legacy `channelId` fallback values on to-mode actions become `channel:` targets unless already prefixed, and adds table tests for Discord and channel-info behavior.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +73. Total +100 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, but not from a live run here. Current main migrates legacy `channelId` into `to` without preserving the `channelId` preferred-kind path, and Discord rejects bare numeric `to` targets unless they are made explicit.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor action is needed because the patch has a compatibility defect and the missing live Discord proof cannot be supplied by a repair worker.

target.title: fix(infra): prefix channelId with channel: when migrating to target
target.body:
## Summary

`normalizeMessageActionInput` migrates legacy `channelId` into `target` for `"to"`-mode message actions such as `read` and `send`. For Discord, a bare numeric snowflake in `target` is ambiguous unless the caller says whether it is a user or channel. The old `channelId` path carried that intent via `preferredKind: "group"`; the migrated `target` path did not.

This PR preserves that intent only when it is safe to do so:

- Bare legacy `channelId` values on `"to"`-mode actions become `channel:<id>`.
- Existing explicit targets such as `channel:<id>`, `group:<id>`, and `discord:channel:<id>` are preserved as-is.
- `"channelId"`-mode actions such as `channel-info` keep using `channelId` as their primary field and are not prefixed.

## Root cause

1. A tool call like `message(action="read", channelId="1507887702379335791")` passes a bare Discord snowflake.
2. Normalization migrates `channelId` into `target`/`to` for the `read` action.
3. Target resolution then processes it through the `to` path, which does not pass `preferredKind: "group"`.
4. Discord cannot disambiguate a bare numeric target and throws `Ambiguous Discord recipient`.

## Validation

Local clean-branch validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```

```text
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

Dicky staging validation:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       18 passed (18)
```

```text
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-normalization.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

## Behavior proof status

Dicky is now running a patched gateway dist with this target-normalization behavior for live Discord proof. The service restarted cleanly and reported Discord ready:

```text
[gateway] ready
[discord] channels resolved: 1488730235544273049/1507887702379335791 (guild:LibraVDB; channel:bots-everywhere), 1488730235544273049/1508635455133057165 (guild:LibraVDB; channel:bot-testing)
```

Pending proof to attach after a live trigger: a redacted after-fix Discord `message(action="read", channelId="1507887702379335791")` run showing no `Ambiguous Discord recipient` error.
